title: build: Create versioned compiler/tooling symlinks in nix environments
body:
<!--
This PR template helps you to write a good pull request description.
Please feel free to include additional useful information even beyond what is requested below.

If your branch is on a personal fork and has a name that allows it to
run CI build/test jobs (e.g. "ci/foo"), remember to rename it BEFORE
opening the PR.  This avoids unnecessary redundant test runs. Renaming
the branch after opening the PR will close the PR.
https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/managing-branches-in-your-repository/renaming-a-branch
-->

## High Level Overview of Change

We don't want people to run `gcc-15` and accidentally get system-installed compiler.
That's why for all tools, which can be version-suffixed we create a symlink.

title: ci: Use rust-overlay to bring Rust into nix
body:
## High Level Overview of Change

The Nix CI image and dev shell now get their Rust toolchain from [`oxalica/rust-overlay`](https://github.com/oxalica/rust-overlay), pinned by the repo's existing `rust-toolchain.toml`, instead of the bare `nixpkgs` `cargo` / `rustc` / `clippy` / `rustfmt` packages. This makes `rust-toolchain.toml` the single source of truth for the Rust version across every environment (nix and the rustup-based non-nix runners), and fixes a `wasmi` build failure in the nix images. A proc-macro smoke test is added to the image build so this class of failure is caught at image-build time.

### Context of Change

Building the in-repo `wasmi/1.0.9` Conan package from source in the `nix-*` images failed (see #5999) while compiling its C-API crate:

    error[E0463]: can't find crate for `wasmi_c_api_macros`

`wasmi_c_api_macros` is a **proc-macro** crate. It compiled fine — the failure is `rustc` being unable to *load* the proc-macro dylib at compile time. A proc-macro is the one Rust artifact type that links `libstd-*.so` dynamically; the RPATH that makes `libstd` resolvable is injected by nixpkgs' cc-wrapper via `NIX_LDFLAGS`, which is only set inside a Nix stdenv. The `wasmi` cargo build runs *outside* a stdenv (Conan → CMake `ExternalProject` → `cargo`), so the proc-macro dylib had no path to `libstd` and `rustc` couldn't load it. This slipped past the image's existing Rust sanity check because that check only builds executables, which statically link std.

Sourcing the toolchain from `rust-overlay` (the upstream Rust binaries, delivered from the Nix store) resolves proc-macro dylib loading without a stdenv, and — because it reads `rust-toolchain.toml` via `rust-bin.fromRustupToolchainFile` — also removes the version drift between `nixpkgs-unstable` and the pinned `1.95` toolchain the non-nix runners already use.

Alternatives considered:

- **`LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$(rustc --print sysroot)/lib` shim** around the cargo build — fixes the sym

If a relevant task or issue, please link it here.
-->
Renames TER codes to updated spec

Renames four Smart Escrow TER codes:

tecWASM_REJECTED -> tecBYTECODE_REJECTED
tefNO_WASM -> tefNO_BYTECODE
tefWASM_FIELD_NOT_INCLUDED -> tefBYTECODE_NOT_INCLUDED
temBAD_WASM -> temINVALID_BYTECODE
Compute->Gas, Wasm->VM, FinishFunction->Bytecode

the branch after opening the PR will close the PR.
https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/managing-branches-in-your-repository/renaming-a-branch
-->

## High Level Overview of Change

Fix workflow launch so matrix-unrelated labels can no longer hide or drop a PR's build. Decisions are now driven purely by the current label set, and a single concurrency group guarantees one authoritative run per ref.

## High Level Overview of Change

- **Concurrency:** collapsed to a single per-ref group with `cancel-in-progress`, so any newer run (push or label change) supersedes the in-progress one. Removes the coexisting-run window where a fast do-nothing run could mask a real build's checks.
- **Decisions from the current label set only:** dropped all `github.event.action` / `github.event.label.name` logic.
  - *Run at all?* — `should-run` runs unless the PR is a draft without `DraftRunCI`/`Full CI build`.
  - *Minimal or full?* — full matrix when `Ready to merge`/`Full CI build` is present, minimal otherwise.
- **Trade-off:** an unrelated label now cancels/re-triggers the build rather than doing nothing — the deliberate cost of never masking real results

<!--

This may be a direct input to the release notes.
If too broad, please consider splitting into multiple PRs.
If a relevant task or issue, please link it here.
-->

### Context of Change

<!--

If a bug fix, when was the bug introduced? What was the behavior?
If a new feature, why was th

title: fix: Re-store nodes missing from both backends during online_delete rotation
body:
## High Level Overview of Change

Fixes a silent durability gap in online delete: a node that is still referenced by the validated state map, but whose only on-disk copy was deleted with a previously rotated-out backend, is never rewritten to disk. The fix has two small, independent layers:

1. **Prevention** — during the rotation window (cache freshening through the backend swap), an ordinary read served by the archive backend is copied forward into the writable backend (`DatabaseRotatingImp::fetchNodeObject`). Today only `duplicate == true` fetches do this; a node fetched from the soon-to-be-deleted archive *after* the freshen snapshot would otherwise survive only in RAM once the archive is dropped.
2. **Repair** — the rotation copy walk (`SHAMapStoreImp::copyNode`) detects a walked node that misses in **both** backends and re-serializes the in-memory body into the writable backend, restoring the invariant that every node reachable from the validated ledger exists on disk.

Both layers mirror existing store patterns (`duplicate` copy-forward and `SHAMap::writeNode` serialize-then-store respectively); each logs on occurrence so operators can see the recovery happen.

## Context of Change

### Symptom

Affected nodes kept dropping in and out of sync with the network. Log analysis tied the desyncs to two surfaces of one condition:

1. **Ledger close**: transactions failed to apply against a missing state-tree node (`SHAMapMissingNode`); the locally built ledger diverged from the network-validated one, taking minutes to recover, repeatedly.
2. **Online delete**: the rotation copy walk halted on the same missing nodes, producing a repeated rotation-abort loop — online delete never completed and disk usage grew, while the continued rotation retries kept the node under load pressure, flapping in and out of sync until a full resync or node reversion.

### Root cause

Verified step by step against the code:

1. The NodeStore is a flat, content-addressed store keyed by node

It checks that the Sponsorship fields are not present when an account root is being deleted. title: ci: Run full matrix only on `Ready to merge` or `Full CI build` labeled PRs
body:
<!--
This PR template helps you to write a good pull request description.
Please feel free to include additional useful information even beyond what is requested below.

If your branch is on a personal fork and has a name that allows it to
run CI build/test jobs (e.g. "ci/foo"), remember to rename it BEFORE
opening the PR.  This avoids unnecessary redundant test runs. Renaming
the branch after opening the PR will close the PR.
https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/managing-branches-in-your-repository/renaming-a-branch
-->

## High Level Overview of Change

Before applying `Ready to merge` label we'll only launch very small subset of configurations.
When someone adds this label, full matrix will be run.
Filters out only labels that change behaviour.
`exclude_event_types` is deleted - it's a over-complication, which doesn't give any visible benefits.
